tests.name: start_phase1_exits_zero
tests.details: process-checklist.sh --start-phase1 returns 0; phase1_architecture object created in process-state.json

tests.name: phase1_steps_array_present
tests.details: .phase1_architecture.steps_completed is array (initially empty)

tests.name: each_phase1_step_completes
tests.details: All 5 PHASE1_STEPS (architecture_selected, threat_model_complete, data_model_defined, ui_scaffolding_done, bible_synthesized) completed sequentially via --complete-step

tests.name: phase1_to_phase2_gate_passes
tests.details: check-phase-gate.sh returns 'Phase gates consistent.' (RC=0) after all phase1 steps complete

tests.name: phase_state_advances_to_2
tests.details: After all phase1 steps complete and check-phase-gate.sh passes, .current_phase remains 0. No script in scripts/ advances current_phase automatically. Confirmed by grepping for 'current_phase = 1' / 'advance.*phase' across scripts/. This is the U-D regression from the prior sweep — still open.

framework_bugs.summary: U-D confirmation: no script advances current_phase after phase gate passes
framework_bugs.reproduction: After completing all phase1_architecture steps, run bash scripts/check-phase-gate.sh; gate reports consistent (RC=0) but jq -r '.current_phase' .claude/phase-state.json still returns 0. Manual jq edit is required to proceed.
framework_bugs.expected: Either check-phase-gate.sh, --complete-step on the final phase1 step, or a dedicated --advance-phase action should bump .current_phase from 0 to 1 (and similarly 1->2, 2->3, 3->4) once gate prerequisites are met.
framework_bugs.actual: .current_phase stays at 0; downstream scripts (check-commit-ready, start_phase4) read it and behave as if Phase 0.

tests.name: start_feature_records_state
tests.details: After --start-feature uat-feat-1: .build_loop.feature = 'uat-feat-1'

tests.name: each_step_completes_in_order
tests.details: All 6 BUILD_LOOP_STEPS (tests_written, tests_verified_failing, implemented, security_audit, documentation_updated, feature_recorded) succeeded sequentially. Security audit artifact requirement enforced — created docs/security-audits/uat-feat-1-security-audit.md to satisfy.

tests.name: pre_commit_passes_after_loop
tests.details: git commit -m 'feat(uat-1): stub feature for UAT' succeeded after 5 build_loop steps complete

tests.name: bl006_blocks_feat_without_loop
tests.details: U-C confirmation: shell-direct 'git commit --allow-empty -m "feat(bl006-trigger): no loop"' completed RC=0 with no Build Loop. The check-commit-message logic in process-checklist.sh works correctly (exits 1 with FAIL message) but is not wired to a git commit-msg hook, so direct shell commits bypass it. PreToolUse hook only catches commits issued through Claude Code's Bash tool.

tests.name: bl006_grace_window_resets_after_record
tests.details: After --record-feature uat-feat-1, started uat-feat-2, completed all 5 build_loop steps, committed feat(uat-2). check-commit-message returned RC=0 for the in-progress loop. PR #17 grace-window fix confirmed.

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_blocks_commit
tests.details: Wrote .claude/pending-approval.json with valid schema. Invoked pre-commit-gate.sh with simulated PreToolUse JSON payload (tool_name=Bash, tool_input.command='git commit ...'). Gate returned permissionDecision: deny with the BL-015 reason ('pending user decision') containing the question/options/recommendation.

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_resolves
tests.details: scripts/pending-approval.sh --resolve removed the sentinel. Subsequent pre-commit-gate.sh invocation no longer mentioned BL-015 (fell through to BL-006 deny because no fresh build loop).

tests.name: recorded_feature_appears_in_progress
tests.details: jq '.features_completed' .claude/build-progress.json contains 'uat-feat-1' and 'uat-feat-2'

framework_bugs.summary: U-C confirmation: BL-006 not enforced for direct shell git commit
framework_bugs.reproduction: From a Phase 2 project with no active build loop, run: git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat(test): no loop'. Commit succeeds RC=0.
framework_bugs.expected: A git commit-msg hook should invoke scripts/process-checklist.sh --check-commit-message and abort the commit when it returns 1.
framework_bugs.actual: Only Claude Code's PreToolUse path triggers the BL-006 check. Direct shell commits bypass it entirely. process-checklist.sh --check-commit-message itself returns the correct exit code (1) when called manually.

documentation_gaps.gap: process-checklist.sh --start-phase4 reads .poc_mode and treats the literal string 'production' as a blocking POC mode, requiring users to run 'upgrade-project.sh --to-production' even on a project initialized with --gov-mode production. This conflates 'POC awaiting upgrade' with 'production target reached'. Either init.sh should not set poc_mode='production' (use null/empty for already-production projects), or start_phase4 should treat 'production' as the unblocked state.
documentation_gaps.where: scripts/process-checklist.sh:533-546 start_phase4(); init.sh poc_mode write path

tests.name: start_phase3_exits_zero
tests.details: process-checklist.sh --start-phase3 RC=0 (with warnings about feature/UAT count)

tests.name: start_uat_creates_session
tests.details: --start-uat 1 set .uat_session.started_at to a non-null ISO timestamp

tests.name: uat_template_lints_clean
tests.details: lint-uat-scenarios.sh state-restatement check (line 111-126) extracts STEPS by reading raw JSON text and uses 'echo $STEPS | head -1', so the first line is the JSON '[' bracket. Even on a properly authored scenario where the first step opens with 'Given the app is open ...', the lint flags 'first line: "["'. This is not core UAT functionality but a pre-existing linter robustness issue.

tests.name: each_uat_step_completes
tests.details: All 9 UAT_STEPS (agents_dispatched .. gate_passed) completed sequentially via --complete-step uat_session:<step>

tests.name: phase3_to_phase4_gate
tests.details: All 9 PHASE3_STEPS completed with stub artifacts in docs/test-results/ (semgrep, accessibility, performance, integration). Gate inconsistencies are due to the absent APPROVAL_LOG.md gate-date entries — a documentation/data issue, not a script bug.

documentation_gaps.gap: lint-uat-scenarios.sh state-restatement check reads the JSON-formatted STEPS array as raw text, so its 'first line' is always the literal '[' bracket. The check effectively cannot pass on any real populated scenario because no step list starts with 'You are', 'cd ', 'Setup:', 'Before starting', or 'Preconditions:' as the first character of the JSON array dump.

tests.name: start_phase4_exits_zero
tests.details: Initial --start-phase4 was blocked because phase-state.json had poc_mode='production'. After running upgrade-project.sh --to-production (which removed poc_mode and set last_upgrade), --start-phase4 succeeded RC=0. Note: upgrade-project also changed .track from 'light' to 'standard' as a side effect — unexpected for a UAT light-track project.

tests.name: each_phase4_step_completes
tests.details: All 6 PHASE4_STEPS (production_build, rollback_tested, go_live_verified, monitoring_configured, handoff_written, handoff_tested) completed with stub artifacts (HANDOFF.md with monitoring section, RELEASE_NOTES.md, docs/test-results/*rollback*, *handoff*).

tests.name: handoff_md_required
tests.details: phase4_release:handoff_written artifact check (line 333-339) correctly required HANDOFF.md before allowing the step

tests.name: release_notes_required
tests.details: phase4_release:go_live_verified required RELEASE_NOTES.md (line 340-346)

tests.name: phase4_completion_state
tests.details: .current_phase = 4; .phase4_release.steps_completed contains all 6 steps

documentation_gaps.gap: upgrade-project.sh --to-production silently changes .track from 'light' to 'standard'. Production projects can be light-track (POCs that ship); the upgrade should not force track change. This isn't strictly U-J (deployment field write) but is a related upgrade-side-effect issue.
documentation_gaps.where: scripts/upgrade-project.sh — to-production code path that writes track='standard'

tests.name: no_uncommitted_secrets
tests.details: grep for password|api[_-]?key|secret|token in committed files matches only framework-shipped docs/templates/scripts (e.g., docs/platform-modules/desktop.md, evaluation-prompts/, scripts/check-versions.sh) discussing those topics — no actual credentials.

tests.name: log_files_consistent
tests.details: .claude/process-audit.log absent — N/A per runbook (no FORCE/reset events occurred)

tests.name: no_orphaned_tempfiles
tests.details: find $WORKDIR/.claude -name '*.tmp' returned nothing

tests.name: no_framework_contamination
tests.details: git -C $FRAMEWORK rev-parse HEAD = d66e8f276079aa352ba90ac54386e1b1896d7494 (unchanged). git status --short shows only pre-existing untracked .claude/ and Reports/uat-2026-04-26/ directories — both present at agent start.

summary: Base/production/desktop/light passed all Section A/D/E/G assertions and most of B/C. Two known-open prior-sweep bugs reconfirmed at Medium (U-D phase advance, U-C BL-006 shell bypass). Two new doc-gap candidates: lint-uat-scenarios state-restatement check mis-reads JSON, and upgrade-project --to-production unexpectedly changes track from light to standard. PR #17/#18/#19 fixes (BL-016 non-interactive, fake-remote tolerance, framework-self guard, BL-015 sentinel via pre-commit-gate, U-H helper copying, BL-006 grace window) all confirmed working.
